summary: Foundation enemies now use three Quaternius CC0 skinned monster GLBs across 12 spaced map-authored enemy markers with skeleton-safe cloning, verified Math.PI model-facing yaw, kinematic patrol, tracking, return-home behavior, debug radius/front cones, larger silhouette hit proxies, visible attached hit flash, separation, health, ray-hit damage, and destruction preserved.
observations: The earlier cube placeholder pass has been superseded by external GLB enemies.
Each external enemy owns a shared Health instance and reports through the debug enemy snapshot.
The first GLB smoke target is destroyable by normal hold-fire input in local browser smoke.
No level-specific external enemy brief has been created.
Visible cube enemies were replaced by Mushnub, Pink Slime, and Goleling GLB visuals from Quaternius via Poly Pizza.
The runtime keeps invisible box hit proxies for weapon ray damage instead of raycasting render meshes.
Enemy asset IDs, byte sizes, hashes, source URLs, and CC0 license records are documented in the source ledger.
The foundation map now uses E for enemy spawn markers and X for the single endpoint marker.
The enemy runtime derives 12 placements from E markers and cycles the current three monster assets across them.
Repeated enemy visuals clone shared loaded GLB templates, so enemy model payload remains 347884B.
Enemies now patrol, detect the player by radius, face the player while tracking, and return to their marker origin after losing range.
Debug mode draws a detection radius and front cone on each living enemy, using red while tracking and green otherwise.
The first smoke target moved to marker row 1, column 12 so it starts patrolling at range instead of instantly clustering beside the player.
Weapon rays now hit a 2.15 x 2.35 x 2.15 invisible enemy proxy and briefly show an additive hit flash on damage.
Living enemies now get a lightweight separation correction so multiple trackers do not stack on the same player-adjacent spot.
Quaternius enemy GLBs are skinned meshes and now use SkeletonUtils.clone for repeated runtime instances.
The hit flash is parented to the animated visual slot so it follows the GLB bob/scale.
Enemy snapshots report attachment anchors and model bounds so detachment can be tested.
Repeatable headed enemy QA now confirms the skinned bodies are visibly present, moving, and attached to their hit/debug proxies.
Headed close-facing QA verified the Quaternius monster model-local yaw should be Math.PI; the Mushnub eyes face the player and align with the red debug cone at that offset.
decisions: Enemy assets must be judged by silhouette, animation coverage, hit proxies, audio hooks, and mobile performance.
Use recurring enemy types for recognition and unique variants for level identity.
Keep the current enemy system as the behavior contract for external enemy assets.
Keep GLB visuals separate from hit proxies for this MVP enemy slice.
Use procedural transform motion for MVP enemy readability until authored animation clips are sourced.
Keep enemy behavior data in runtime code for now; keep enemyManifest.ts as source/license/model metadata only.
Use map-authored E markers as the source of truth for exact enemy spawn coordinates.
Keep enemy-to-enemy overlap handling as a lightweight runtime separation pass until authored navigation or avoidance is needed.
Keep SkeletonUtils.clone for skinned enemy GLB reuse; do not return to raw Object3D.clone(true) for these assets.
Keep the shared Quaternius monster forward yaw at Math.PI unless future per-asset visual QA proves a specific monster needs a separate offset.
caughtIssues: Current external enemies validate damage and destruction only; they do not block player movement or animate authored hit/death states.
Enemy GLBs still lack authored attack, hit, and death animations; current motion is procedural transform animation only.
Future asset imports must preserve separate hit proxies so combat does not depend on mesh raycasts.
The earlier first marker was too close to spawn and made the opening read as enemy overlap or stuck behavior.
The original hit proxy was too narrow for the external silhouettes, making some player-visible shots feel like misses.
Raw Object3D.clone(true) produced valid-looking bounds while the skinned enemy body did not render reliably in headed QA.
Single-frame movement assertions can false-fail on looping patrols; use the headed QA runner's sampled movement window.
The initial 180-degree guess from the old Math.PI / 2 yaw still showed side/back views; direct close-facing screenshot comparison showed Math.PI is the correct shared yaw.
risks: Enemy assets without readable attack and hit states will make mobile combat feel unfair.
Replacing cubes with external assets can break ray-hit readability if hit proxies are not authored deliberately.
Static enemy models may read as props without later animation, hit flash, or death feedback.
Shrinking the current enemy hit proxy can reintroduce visible-hit misses on mobile.
Future skinned enemy assets can appear detached or invisible if cloned without SkeletonUtils.

currentEnemyAssets.movementModel: kinematic patrol/tracking/return-home with transform-only visual motion
currentEnemyAssets.debugVisuals: detection radius plus front cone, red while tracking and green otherwise
currentEnemyAssets.hitFeedback: brief additive sphere flash on successful damage, parented to the enemy visual slot
